content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all four changed files (model.rs, bundle.rs, service.rs, project.rs) end-to-end. The pending-lineage-reset crash-recovery mechanism is well-designed: it records the deferred reset in the snapshot, retries on any subsequent mutation entry point (materialize_bundle, record_bead_start, update_task_run, repair_task_run), and self-heals from partial failures including missing or corrupted plan.shape.json. The implicit-slot-matching refinement and acceptance-criteria trimming are correct. Tests are thorough, covering the failure/retry path, self-healing from each entry point, and the membership_confirmed behavioral change. The one substantive finding is duplicated implicit-slot-matching logic across bundle.rs and project.rs that could silently diverge.

## Amendments

1. Extract shared implicit-slot-matching logic into a single function usable by both `bundle.rs` and `project.rs`. Currently `bead_matches_implicit_slot` (bundle.rs:429) and `implicit_slot_matches` (project.rs:899) implement identical logic with slightly different parameter types (`&str` vs `&MilestoneId`). If one is modified without the other, bead-resolution behavior silently diverges between canonicalization and runtime bead-plan resolution—a class of bug that is hard to catch since the two code paths are exercised by different test suites.
   Rationale: This is a correctness risk, not just style. The two functions gate whether a bead is treated as explicit-id vs implicit-slot, which in turn controls whether progress is preserved across plan revisions and whether membership_confirmed is set. A divergence would produce different canonicalized shapes at plan-persist time vs different resolution results at bead-start time, leading to silently wrong flow_override or lost progress.
